Official renewal paths

1. Renew online — if eligible
Official page: https://travel.state.gov/en/passports/renew-replace/online.html
Official online portal: https://opr.travel.state.gov

You can renew online if all apply:
- The passport was valid for 10 years.
- It is expiring within 1 year, or expired less than 5 years ago.
- You are age 25 or older.
- You are not changing personal information such as name or sex.
- You are not traveling for at least 6 weeks from the date you submit.
- You are located in a U.S. state or territory when submitting.
- You have the passport, it is not damaged/mutilated, and it was not reported lost or stolen.
- You want routine service. Online renewals cannot be expedited at submission.
- You are renewing the same document type: book-to-book, card-to-card, or book+card-to-book+card. If adding a first book/card, use mail instead.

Online steps:
- Go only to https://opr.travel.state.gov.
- Gather current passport, digital passport photo, SSN/emergency contact info, and credit/debit card.
- Complete your own application; third parties cannot submit it for you.
- Pay fee:
  - Passport book: $130
  - Passport card: $30
  - Book + card: $160
  - Optional 1–3 day delivery: $22.05
- Do not mail the old passport. Keep it, but do not use it for travel after submitting; State will cancel/invalidate it.
- Track status by email or passportstatus.state.gov.

2. Renew by mail — if eligible
Official page: https://travel.state.gov/en/passports/renew-replace/mail.html

You can renew by mail if:
- You can submit your most recent U.S. passport with the application.
- It has never been reported lost or stolen.
- It was issued within the last 15 years.
- It was issued when you were age 16 or older and was valid for 10 years.
- It is in your current name, or you can provide certified legal name-change evidence.
- It is not damaged, except normal wear and tear.

You cannot renew a child’s passport under age 16; children must apply for a new passport.

Mail steps:
- Complete Form DS-82, preferably using the official Form Filler.
- Print single-sided; sign and date it.
- Include your most recent passport.
- If changing name, include a certified copy of marriage certificate, divorce decree, or court order.
- Include 1 passport photo stapled as instructed.
- Pay by personal check or money order payable to “U.S. Department of State”; do not send cash.
- Fees:
  - Passport book: $130
  - Passport card: $30
  - Book + card: $160
  - Expedited service: add $60
  - Optional 1–3 day return delivery: add $22.05
- Mail via USPS to the correct address:
  - Routine service, if you live in CA, FL, IL, MN, NY, or TX:
    National Passport Processing Center
    Post Office Box 640155
    Irving, TX 75064-0155
  - Routine service, all other states:
    National Passport Processing Center
    Post Office Box 90155
    Philadelphia, PA 19190-0155
  - Expedited service:
    Write “EXPEDITE” on the envelope and mail to:
    National Passport Processing Center
    Post Office Box 90955
    Philadelphia, PA 19190-0955

Processing guidance

Official page: https://travel.state.gov/en/passports/apply/help/processing-time.html

Current official guidance says:
- Routine processing: 4–6 weeks.
- Expedited processing: 2–3 weeks.
- Mailing is not included in processing time.
- Add up to 2 weeks for the application to reach State.

What you should verify next
